subject: Gaming
title: Nintendo 3DS: The Comeback Console
description: A fast breakdown of Nintendo 3DS: The Comeback Console for builders.

hook.text: The 3DS launched at $250 and flopped so badly that Nintendo slashed the price by $80 in six months.

buildup.text: Glasses-free 3D was the gimmick. Early buyers found few games worth playing in three dimensions.

discovery.text: Pokémon X and Y, Animal Crossing, and Fire Emblem revived the system. Great games saved bad launches.

twist.text: Nintendo apologized to early buyers with 20 free games—the 'Ambassador Program.' A rare corporate apology.

climax.text: The 3DS sold 76 million units. It proved Nintendo could recover from disaster with the right games.

punchline.text: A terrible launch. A price cut. Then Pokémon saved everything.

quiz.question: How did Nintendo recover from the 3DS's poor launch?
options: A major price cut and strong exclusive games like Pokémon
